summary: Cryptocurrency supplemental lecture material (page 49 excerpt) — unreadable (pdftoppm not available on this system).

MultiCharts PowerLanguage strategy (DLHHL): volatility-adaptive trend-following system using day-high/low pivot references and rolling N-day average volatility ratios to set dynamic entry thresholds, with graduated position scaling and timed exit near session close.

MultiCharts PowerLanguage strategy (HLM): breakout system that tracks the rolling N-day highest-high and lowest-low midpoint, entering long when today's range shifts higher than yesterday's and short when it shifts lower, with incremental add-on entries and a staged timed exit.

MultiCharts PowerLanguage strategy (MA): dual-moving-average crossover system (5-period vs 60-period) that enters long during day session or overnight when fast MA > slow MA and price exceeds session high average, and shorts only when price is below 5-day closing average, with settlement-day forced close.

MultiCharts PowerLanguage strategy (ma+uvdv): extends the MA crossover system by adding a UVDV composite signal (spread between data2 and data3, likely up-volume vs down-volume) as a filter, dynamically adjusting MA periods based on win-count history for long and short sides separately.

MultiCharts PowerLanguage strategy (maimai_fish): mean-reversion DCA system that places an initial limit buy below the 60-bar moving average by N points, then adds two more limit buy layers each 300 points lower, exiting on a 300-point profit from any layer or on settlement day.

MultiCharts PowerLanguage strategy (MXNDU): volatility-normalized breakout using rolling N-day average of max/min daily distances from computed pivot levels to calibrate entry thresholds, with bar-count-gated stop-loss and trailing take-profit exits after minimum holding period.

MultiCharts PowerLanguage strategy (RushBear): short-side momentum system based on session open vs historical high-open volatility ratio, entering short when price breaks below a pivot computed from session open minus trend-volatility; uses MFE-based profit target and time-gated stop-loss tied to next-session open.

MultiCharts PowerLanguage strategy (SBF core): gap-fade mean-reversion system entering long when open < prior close minus N-day avg range and price breaks day high, short vice-versa; exits via 0.5×volRange stop and limit orders with forced market close at session end.

MultiCharts PowerLanguage strategy (SBF_1): adaptive trend-following system computing intra-bar slope from 20-day ATR divided by day length, tracking intraday up/down adjustment scores to dynamically shift long/short price thresholds, with trend carry-over across sessions and momentum-based stop triggers.

MultiCharts PowerLanguage strategy (SBF_20220306): full production SBF with 20-day rolling distance arrays tracking pivot min/max distances, separate long/short start prices, intra-bar up/down adjustment counters, dual lot entries with stop-based add-on, and a staged 5-bar exit ramp.

MultiCharts PowerLanguage strategy (SBF_ADA): asymmetric slope-filter system using separate bearish (C1/C4) and bullish (C2/C3) bar-count accumulators to gate long/short entries relative to an intraday slope threshold derived from 20-day ATR, with settlement-day and overnight session guards.

MultiCharts PowerLanguage strategy (SBF_dev): experimental version computing average trend/correction velocities per bar over 20 days for bull/bear days separately, uses parabolic (k-squared) position thresholds for a dynamic box boundary, with half-position partial exit when price reverses through the box.

MultiCharts PowerLanguage strategy (SBF_Ori, original): simplified 1-minute SBF kernel using min/max of open vs prior close as start boundaries and ATR-scaled slope times bar-count for long/short thresholds; clean session-end exit without arrays or adjustment counters.

MultiCharts PowerLanguage strategy (SBF_ori2): extends SBF_Ori by adding dual intraday dn/upAdj counters that shift threshold prices each bar based on candle structure; uses same 5-bar staged exit ramp with commented-out prior stop logic preserved.

MultiCharts PowerLanguage strategy (SBF_RushBear_chg): short-biased variant merging RushBear short-entry logic with SBF session-open/high tracking for long entries; uses MFE-adjusted stop for shorts, 5-bar staged exit, and has long lots disabled by default (lotL1=lotL2=0).

PowerLanguage utility snippet tracking rolling 100-trade arrays of MAE and MFE normalized by ATR entry range for both long and short sides, computing 3-trade running averages as a strategy performance diagnostics helper.

summary: PowerLanguage short-entry condition snippet library (c_entry = ...) cataloguing ~80 distinct short-side entry conditions used across intraday futures strategies, covering RSI, MACD, DMI, pivot, volatility, MA-cross, and session-open-gap filters.

PowerLanguage strategy building-block snippets covering ATR-based stops, Keltner breakout, Bollinger Band entry, MACD/Momentum/Stochastic long-entry templates, and standard MultiCharts built-in strategy code patterns for LE/LX orders.

Trading framework architecture document describing entry/exit structure (LE, SE, LX, SX symmetry), exit categories (large-move exits, gradual exits), exit mechanics (price reference, time-based, composite), and data channel taxonomy for position tracking.

Development architecture reference document for the algo-trading platform, describing entry/exit signal symmetry, position-tracking variable taxonomy (entryprice, MFE, MAE, PnL arrays), time-based and composite exit logic layers, and secondary data channel (volume, OI) integration.

Python SQLite data-access class (StgSql) for strategy aggregation database, wrapping connect/execute/commit/close lifecycle, with reset/delete/insert/query methods for stg_position and stg_agg_* tables used in portfolio-level P&L tracking.

Verbatim transcript of a multi-session algorithmic trading fundamentals course (basic class), covering what systematic trading is, mindset requirements, expected cost structure, and introductory portfolio performance review for students new to program trading.

Large JSON-encoded library of numbered MultiCharts PowerLanguage intraday strategy bodies (60+ strategies, 5K–300K score range), each a self-contained LE/SE/LX/SX block with ATR-based stop/profit logic, settlement-day exit guards, and diverse entry conditions (volatility-band, MA-cross, pivot, MACD, box-breakout, etc.).

Presentation slides from the 2024-03-30 KaiWei (Kaivest) meetup gathering — unreadable (pdftoppm not available on this system).

PowerLanguage intraday strategy (9K bar) from the AK portfolio: uses a multi-condition momentum scoring system (SizeBuy/SizeSell) based on bar structure and open vs. close comparisons to scale entry size, with stop-loss scaled to entries-today and asymmetric take-profit ratios (2x buy, 6.5x sell), plus day-end forced flat and settlement-day exit.

Short mean-reversion/pair-relative strategy (PowerLanguage): calculates Z-scores of the primary instrument and a secondary data feed (Data2) using rolling standardization, enters long when primary Z-score crosses above secondary and is sufficiently positive, short when it crosses below, exits at session end.

Simple dual-MA + MACD trend-following strategy (PowerLanguage): buys when close is above both 80- and 120-bar MAs with positive MACD histogram, fixed take-profit/stop-loss in points, with a re-entry suppression flag after a successful take-profit until the trend condition resets.

30K-bar MA + MACD breakout strategy (PowerLanguage) with a sophisticated three-tier stop management: initial stop based on range barrier, breakeven stop after partial profit, and trailing stop once max profit exceeds a threshold, with configurable barrier multipliers and asymmetric buy/sell barrier widths.

15K-bar pivot-based reversal strategy (PowerLanguage, EA_Key_Condition gated): uses PivotLowVS/PivotHighVS functions with a configurable lookback period to identify pivot turning points, enters at the highest low or lowest open with asymmetric stop-loss values, no overnight holding.

60K-bar pivot breakout strategy (PowerLanguage): identifies pivot highs and lows using symmetric lookback, enters long on breakout above highest close or short below lowest close, with tight fixed point stops (75 buy, 25 sell), no overnight holding.

ATR-breakout momentum strategy (PowerLanguage): long entry when price exceeds MA plus ATR multiplied by NB/10 with a short-MA cross over longer-MA condition, short entry symmetric; settlement-day auto-exit; uses separate bar types (open/high/low/close) for buy vs. sell price references.

Dual-EMA difference crossover strategy (PowerLanguage) for intraday and overnight sessions: computes difference between 2x-EMA and 3x-EMA, buys on cross above threshold and shorts below negative threshold; exits on adverse EMA sign reversal combined with price action (low-stop for longs, high-stop for shorts).

Multi-day open-price average entry strategy (PowerLanguage): stores the last N days' opening prices in an array, compares current close to their average at session end to determine direction, uses trailing stop and fixed stop-loss with asymmetric risk for long vs. short positions.
CSV file named 'big_user_trade_record.csv' in the AK directory — likely a large user trade history dataset associated with the AK trading system; content not read directly (binary/large CSV).

path: E:\@交易\@程式交易\ALGO 知名不具\00V_Alexander_Pagonidis_The-IBS-Effect-Mean-Reversion-in-Equity-ETFs-1.pdf

summary: AK system strategy optimization guide (Markdown): covers key optimization principles — sufficient sample variety (trend/oscillation/bull/bear), meaningful parameter steps, and stepwise convergence — plus common pitfalls (curve-fitting, overly complex filters, over-tuning), parameter sensitivity analysis, brute-force vs. genetic algorithm tradeoffs, and the axiom that a valid strategy should profit without heavy optimization.

AK system developer scratch notes: references 15 strategy groups, mentions two lost indicator plot lines (vMA±vMaBuy/vMaSell for an AK05 indicator and EA lost-horse plots), and shows two short code snippets for dynamic stop-loss visualization using plt() based on current market position.

AVAVA automated trading system resource-threshold configuration: sets GDI/CPU/RAM warning thresholds at 68%, defines system notification window (08:15–01:45) and quote notification window (08:45–01:45) with a midday exclusion (13:45–15:00) matching Taiwan futures market hours.

AVAVA automated futures-rollover reminder schedule (CSV): a chronological list of scheduled messages from 2021/07 through 2023/06 that trigger at 14:00 on each Taiwan futures expiration date, prompting the operator to execute the contract rollover procedure for TWTX contracts.

AVAVA strategy registry JSON: defines a strategy schema template with fields for system name, commodity (TX), period, enable/disable mode, stop threshold, XDD/MDD equity limits, heartbeat sync time, and position metadata; the only populated entry is a placeholder 'ATM00' strategy showing the expected data structure.

AVAVA system authentication config: stores login credentials for l12203685@gmail.com with an obfuscated/encrypted password, a long encrypted cookie string, and a hardcoded AuthCode GUID — indicating this is a saved session for the AVAVA proprietary trading platform.

Portfolio management strategy allocation list: maps seven strategy identifiers (CF11–CF20) each with an equal weight of 1, representing a uniform-weighted portfolio configuration for an automated trading system managing multiple CF-series strategies.

DP (Dynamic Portfolio) configuration file for the 05:00 session: assigns TXS83 strategy a weight of 0.05, indicating a 5% allocation within the dynamic portfolio management framework at the early morning session.

MP (Master Portfolio) configuration file: assigns TXS83 strategy a weight of 0.5, indicating a 50% allocation within the master portfolio — significantly higher than the DP session weight, reflecting different risk sizing between portfolio layers.

path: E:\@交易\@程式交易\ALGO 知名不具\FDG_Seminar_150408.pdf

summary: Bohun 2020-07 exchange: MA+ATR dual-mode entry on 29m TXF — ATR-stable breakout entry when volatility low, counter-trend entry when ATR spikes; stop at recent low or entry minus ATR.

ChienShen 047: Correlation Trend on 13K FITXI — momentum indicator gates entry when threshold exceeded and rising vs prior bar; four exits: dynamic low, no-new-high, session-end loss exit, monthly stop; WF via ROA.
JohnsonLo 011: Range-comparison trend on 19m FITXI — compares amplitude of prior vs next len-bar windows, uses Williams %R for trend direction, pullback entry at dynamic range high/low.

TimChen 003: Keltner Channel breakout on 30m TXF full session — enters long when midline trends up and price breaks upper band; exits include stop, profit target, and 9% daily circuit breaker at 9% range.

WenZiGi TopBot 1-6: Gap-and-reversal on 6m TXF day session — classifies 4 open-gap scenarios (gap x trend direction), reversal confirmation entry before 11:00, single trade per day, exit near prior close after N bars.

path: E:/@交易/@StrategyManagement/歸檔/202007/202007Kuang/202007 QTC_TX_SAR_Kuang_DO_6K 策略說明.txt
summary: Kuang SAR: Parabolic SAR + gap-up pre-condition on 6m TXF 09:15-10:30 entry window; buy stop at Length-bar high; exits on fixed point stop or dynamic trailing low; hard flat at 13:39 and settlement day.

YenShen 001: CCI breakout trend on 28m FITXI — stop-buy at N-bar high when CCI exceeds overbought zone; trailing stop at N-bar low or no-new-high detection; settlement hard exit; two exit speed modes.
shuenhua TX_Pivot_DO: Classic pivot-point intraday on 9m TXF — H1/H2/H3/L1/L2/L3 from prior-day HLC, 3-tier entry with 3-contract scaling; capital constraint filter 1.5M; partially garbled encoding.

WGN 123: High-low opening range breakout on 3m TXF — entry after price exceeds prior session high/low by in*0.5 bar threshold; today's high/low as boundary exit; re-entry filter prevents same-side repeat.

Morton No22: Cumulative price-change RSI on 7m TXF intraday — bar-level changes accumulated into custom RSI, long entry on oversold break with ATR-based stop; partially garbled but entry/exit logic confirmed readable.

Keltner channel breakout strategy on TX 13m bars. Long-only (多單). Entry: price breaks above Keltner band near session open. Exit: trailing 180pt stop. WF params: KLen=14, KAtr=3.6-4.4, STL=70-150, Gap=140-220. Cost 1000/RT.

Multi-entry trend following on TXF full session 6K bars. Strategy D027_6KAM_MID_TREND_DO. 4 entry mechanisms: (1) candle formation breakout on trend side, (2) pullback reentry above trend pivot, (3)/(4) continuation after pullback. 4 exit types: 100pt profit target, high/low boundary, time-based at 3hr/3.5hr, dynamic trailing 500pt drawdown. WF by 1hr/2hr EMA crosses. No external WF needed.

Linear regression dual-MA crossover on FITX 5min. Strategy 002_Sw_D_5min_Linear. LR1=raw linear regression, LR2=smoothed LR. Entry when LR diff > N threshold AND consecutive N bars increasing AND close > LR2. Also trades intraday relative high/low breakouts. SL=55pt. WF stable 2016-2020 with params len=16, N=13, sl=55.

Three-price level breakout on TXF 5min. Strategy G003_TX_LT_3Prices. Three reference lines: ML(mid), HL(high band), LL(low band). Entry conditions: no uptrend, no existing long exit signal, price not at last high. Long when price > HL with expanding candle or contracting candle. Exit: price vs band relationship, trailing stop at win_pts*(1-tb), fixed STP stop. WF 2013-2020.

3-high 3-low breakout long-only on TXA 1H. Strategy H054_TXA_3H3L_LO_1H. Entry: 3-bar high minus 3-bar low > H3L3 threshold (180pt), enter at lowest low of recent Enlen bars. Exit: SL=170pt hard stop, PF=840pt profit target, MaxPP=360pt trailing drawdown reversal. WF stable 2014-2020. Overnight hold allowed.

Box breakout with RSI filter on TXF 5min (long-only 多單). Strategy 009_TX_Box_FDT0_Jack. Main logic: time-box breakout with RSI momentum confirmation. 3 entry modes: standard breakout, counter-entry after 2-bar low, re-entry after SL with RSI still bullish. 5 exit modes: personal trailing, fixed SL, time-based, fixed profit, EOD. Drawdown control: skip entries if DD > 75000. ADR time filter to avoid anomalies.

Keltner Channel + CCI breakout on FITX 30min. Strategy khsu_CFExTest_202010_KcCCI. Entry: price above KC upper band AND CCI > 100 → long; price below KC lower band AND CCI < -100 → short. Avoid first and last bar entries. Simple 2-condition system WF by net profit. Exit: price crosses back through KC bands.

MACD crossover with Keltner trend filter on TXF 5min (long-only 廣進). Strategy CL012_TX_MACD_LO. Entry1: MACD cross up while MACD < 0 and close > open → long. Entry2: if entry1 SL hit, re-enter on reversal (max 8 consecutive tries). Exit: trailing stop = ATR multiple OR 7% or 500pt hard cap, EOD close. Params mostly fixed, minor WF adjustments 2017-2020.

Three-day high-low range breakout on TXF 60min (long-only 趨勢L進). Strategy 202010HW. Inspired by blog strategy. Entry: range breakout from dday-period high/low by gap pts. Exit1: trailing stop at 20% pullback from entry+stp. Exit2: fixed SL at stp pts. Exit3: EOD close. WF stable 2015-2020 with stp=100, gap=50, dday=10.

Bollinger Band breakout with momentum confirmation on TXF 30min. Strategy 513_Bollinger_band. Entry: 3 consecutive bars breaking upper band with expanding range → long; 3 bars below lower band with expanding range AND previous trade not losing short → short. Exit1: close touches midband. Exit2: if profit > threshold use outer bands as exit. Exit3: time-based after breakout bars.

Time-filter channel breakout on FITX 5min (日盤當沖). Strategy 045_TX_BY_DO. Logic: enter long at daily high pivot, enter short at daily low pivot, tracked across mp=0 session reset. Exit: fixed bars, daily pivot cross, EOD. Parameterized len/aa/BB/notin with walk-forward date overrides from 2014 to 2020. Cost $600, max 1 contract.

Candlestick shadow ratio strategy on TXA 60min (long-only). Strategy No25_TXA_CandleShadow_60K_LO. Logic: identifies bars whose lower shadow exceeds a ratio of prior N-bar range (LowerLen, Ratio) as potential reversal pivots; filters via upper shadow confirmation (UpperLen). Entry at next candle close confirming direction. Exit: low of entry bar as SL. WF 2013-2020, cost $1000.

3-day high-low range breakout on TX 7min (long-only 趨勢). Strategy 020_TX_3DAYBRK_LO. Entry: ADX confirms trending, prior 3-day H-L range > 80pts → long on 3-day high break, short on 3-day low break. Exit1: time-based trailing using 60pt threshold. Exit2: re-entry after pullback to LL/HH. Exit3: 2% trailing, 30% recovery. Exit4: EOD close. Inspired by Corey 10-stock framework.

Triple confirmation trend-follow on TX 13min (long-only 日盤). Strategy 010_3G_D. Core: 3 trend filters must align — momentum expansion, breakout bar geometry, counif time-based trend count. Entry: only one trade per day allowed at first valid bar. Exit1: trailing 30pt stop. Exit2: ATR dynamic. Exit3: pattern reversal. Unique counif logic prevents overtrading in choppy sessions.

Two-week momentum intraday strategy on FITX 3min (日盤當沖). Strategy TX_TwoWeek_DO. Entry L1 (main): MTM momentum + position above Keltner band in 0930-1045 window. Entry L2 (secondary): ATR channel expansion in 0900-1100 window. Re-entry L2 after drawdown <100pts. Exit: 30pt fixed stop, ATR trailing after 2nd entry, Keltner breach, 1330 forced close. Max 2 entries/day.

CDP pivot-based momentum day-trade on TXF 1min (日盤當沖). Strategy P_CDP_TXF_1_DT_V2. Uses CDP as bias control; records 0845-0930 range. Long if gap-up open AND price breaks 0930 high; short if gap-down AND price breaks 0930 low. Exits: fixed profit SP (180), fixed SL, max-profit drawback RT (160), EOD close. Clean 3-parameter design with high R:R bias.

Linear regression angle crossover trend-follow on TXF 16min full-session (波段). Strategy 006_TXA_13mLineReg_LOS_V2. Entry: LinReg curve trending up AND crosses moving average AND angle > p2 threshold → long. Exit1: LinReg trend reversal (angle < p1). Exit2: 600pt profit target. Exit3: 9% daily limit. Exit4: fixed SL. Exit5: EOD. Cost $1000, evolved from June homework.

Aroon indicator breakout with stop-and-reverse on TX 29min (全日盤). Strategy CBST020_Aroon_29m_FLO. Entry long: Aroon_up >= 100 → buy at next bar high with stop. Entry short: Aroon_dn >= 100 → sell at next bar low with stop. Exit1: fixed SL. Exit2: Aroon_up drops below arb threshold AND Aroon oscillator/trend weakens. Reference: YouTube Aroon tutorial.

Moving average channel breakout on TXF 13min (日盤). Strategy unnamed (len/width/sl parameters). Entry: price breaks above MA+width*ATR band → long. Entry filter: price must be at/above MA. Exit: ATR trailing stop = lowest low entry. Walk-forward with date splits. Core idea: MA band acts as dynamic support/resistance; enter on momentum expansion beyond band. Cost $600.

60-min MA channel long-only on TX (全日盤). Strategy 60MAL0. Entry: uses HL ratio threshold vs prior bar low to identify breakout pivots; entry on HL-filtered bar confirmation. Exit: previous bar's low as trailing stop. Walk-forward HL parameter 0.0038→0.005 across quarterly dates 2014-2020. Simple momentum + channel filter. Cost $1000.

path: E:\@交易\@程式交易\ALGO 知名不具\IchimokuKinkoHyo.pdf

summary: High-low band pivot breakout on TXF 45min (long-only 波段). Strategy 019_TX_LongBandForwardTrade_LO. Logic: identifies new high/low pivots from prior K-bars with threshold multiplier; uses ATR-normalized band to confirm breakout strength before entry. Entry: band confirmed upper/lower breakout with ATR ratio filter and reverse-K filter. Exit1: band breach after profit; Exit2: pivot-based trailing. WF 2013-2020 with Action (0.016-0.023) and Stoploss (210-250) parameters.

4-condition convergence day-trade on TXA 5min (日盤當沖). Strategy 017_TXA_CO4_DO_5min. 4 entry conditions: CO4 pattern (4-bar convergence to single line), minimum bar separation before reentry, strong close confirmation. Exits: high pivot breach + defined distance, Keltner band breach, consolidation with 200pt+ gain exit 20% recovery, PS3/4/5 parameter variants for fine-tuning. WF N1(4-16), N2(2-14), N3(2-14), pHL_lens(2-52). Cost 300 entry, 600 round-trip.

path: E:/@交易/@StrategyManagement/歸檔/202010/202010Jack軒邑/202010作業_009_TX_Box_FDT0_Jack策略說明.txt
summary: Gap fill + RSI box breakout day-trade on TXF 5min (日盤). Strategy 009_TX_Box_FDT0_Jack. Core: uses ADR gap filter to avoid late entries, Box boundary (SLength, tt) defines trade range. Entry1: main gap direction break. Entry2: prior 2-bar low minus 1pt reverse. Entry3: prior RSI exit + large ATR breakout. Exits: personal SL, pivot stop, time-based, EOD. Drawdown control 75K. WF 2013/01-2020/08, SLength 5-13, tt 1-16, cc 3-18. Cost $600.

Gap-fill + trend-follow hybrid day-trade on TXFM 6min (日盤當沖). Strategy 007_TXFM_COVERGAP_D0_01. 8 entry signals: small gap up→contrarian short, mid gap up→contrarian (no new high), large gap up→trend long, no-gap early trend long, and symmetrical short variants for gap down. Exits: gap fill target, profit zone, counter-trend reversal signals (HURT UP/DOWN), jump gap reversal (JUMP), profit save, H2/L2 daily level, EOD. WF R1(30-38), R2(37-52), jump(58-83). Cost $600.

Upper/lower band price channel with midline reversal on TXF 10min (日盤當沖). Strategy 907-breakout-flip (辰軍). Core: dynamic upper/lower ban calculated from prior close + highgap/lowgap (ATR-like relative to mid). Entry: H>upperban with multi-filter (H>prior high or open relationship to mid, OHLC average > mid) → long. Night-session flip: touches mid → reverse position. Time-based EOD exit. Post-2017 night session handling with date-split at 1170515. WF x(0-40), in(5-35). Cost $600.

path: E:/@交易/@StrategyManagement/歸檔/202010/202010阿信/07.202010功課004_TX_KDHL_LT策略說明檔.txt
summary: KD-triggered H/L breakout long-only swing on TX 60min (全日盤). Strategy 004_TX_KDHL_LT. Entry: SlowK>80 for 3+ consecutive bars → overbought confirmed trend; SlowK>SlowD AND K < in-filter → buy at prior daily high stop. Exit1: SlowK<20 for 3 bars (trend exhaustion). Exit2: KD cross after KD<20, trailing pivot stop. Exit3: price retreats to lowD(0). Exit4: EOD. WF Length(7-13), in(2-6). Cost $1000, max 300 bars.

path: E:/@交易/@StrategyManagement/歸檔/202010/202010彥勝-卡滋卡滋/彥勝-202010作業-策略說明.txt
summary: Aroon-100 breakout long-only on TXAM 36min (日盤). Strategy 002_TXAM_Aroon_LO. Entry: for-loop Aroon computation over len bars; Aroon_up=100 at bar → buy stop 1pt above prior high. Exit1: Aroon_up ≤ Floor_PT threshold → market exit. Exit2: trailing pullback PT_lost(100) from peak. Exit3: fixed SL at entry - PT_lost. Exit4: next-day gap check if reverse gap + black candle → exit. Settlement day handled. WF Len(39-99), PT_lost(70-130). Cost $1000.

Pivot-based momentum long/short on TXF 6min (日盤當沖). Strategy 001_TX_PT_D0. Entry: if current bar close is above/below prior bar by G-ratio (G×ATR threshold), confirm directional momentum → buy/sell next bar. Filter: ratio G compared to prior-bar high/low relationship. WF G(0.3-0.8) quarterly 2014-2020 with G settling at 0.6 post-2015. Exits: time-based, reversal, EOD. Cost $600, max 50 bars.

Breakout-flip range strategy on TX 5min (日盤波段). Strategy 907-破底翻 (涂旭帆). Entry: price drops below N-bar range then reverses back inside range → contrarian long (breakout-flip). Range defined by lan(18) bars; body ratio ratio3(2.7) filters real vs. shadow candles. Exits: breach of range bottom, extreme candle exit, settlement day exit. Symmetric for short side. WF lan(15-31), ratio3(1.8-3). Cost $1000.

ATR-filtered contrarian day-trade on TX 300min full session (波段). Strategy 027_TX_DayContrarian_LO_300M. Entry long: 3 K-bar reversal inside monthly/DD level limits; two sub-filters: ATRratio×ATR22 body size check OR 3-bar 3% recovery confirmation. Exit: reversal signal + large pivot breach (Ϥ�), SL, EOD. Monthly profit limit 150K, max DD 200K. WF ATRratio(0.8-1.2) quarterly 2014-2020, settling at 0.95-1.0. Cost $1000.

Strategy exchange review for Feb 2021. Individual feedback on ~40+ members strategy submissions (Abner, Alex, Casper, etc.). Notes on parameter sensitivity issues, fixed parameters needing attention, and format compliance for strategy description files.

Strategy exchange review for Jun 2021. Reviews ~35 members across 4+ tiers. Common issues: parameter sensitivity too low, format non-compliance in description files. Edward noted for format issues in parameter docs. Steven Yeh praised for standard parameter range selection.

Strategy exchange review for Jul 2021. Reviews ~30 members in multiple tiers. Issues flagged: low parameter sensitivity (Hsiang, Jack Wang), unused parameters in sensitivity analysis (samuel), ROBUST2 issues (漫漫的). Edward (spelled "Edware") marked OK.

Strategy exchange review for Aug 2021. Reviews ~25 members. Key issues: insufficient time in last period (K.O), parameter range errors corrected (LoveVan), low sensitivity on first parameter (吳憲宗). Blue and NICK flagged for fixed parameter concerns.

Strategy exchange review for Sep 2021. Reviews ~25 members. Edward noted for EXCEL format issues. Steven Yeh praised for simplified sensitivity analysis. Multiple members flagged for low parameter sensitivity or fixed parameters (Sean Wang, 志成, 許景帆).

Strategy exchange review for Oct 2021 (ALL members). Reviews ~20 members. Bignose praised for excellent documentation with diagrams. Hsuanpin warned about profit concentration in last 2 years (adaptability concern). Ren noted as a parameter-free strategy.

Strategy exchange review for Nov 2021 (ALL). Reviews ~20 members. Hsiang warned about negative WF in last period. Yita flagged for uniformly low parameter sensitivity. Oscar questioned fixed parameter usage. Ren continues with parameter-free strategy.

Strategy exchange review for Dec 2021 (ALL). Reviews ~18 members. Casper had chart value errors. Edward advised to expand first parameter range. NaiWeiCheng praised as reference standard for sensitivity testing. Shuen hua reminded to use 5+ parameter sample points.

AVAVA strategy HLORB01: TAIEX futures day-trading on 3-min bars. Entry on breakout of recent high/low point with gap filter + time filter (before 1PM). Exit on day high/low or session end. Walk-forward optimized 2013-2020.
